title: Kapitel 10 - Tabellen Check
description: Wissenscheck:  Grundaufbau von Tabellen mit table, tr, td und th.

byOptionId.table: Correct â€” <table> is the container for rows and cells.
byOptionId.tr: Incorrect â€” <tr> defines a table row within <table>.
byOptionId.td: Incorrect â€” <td> is a table cell, not the table root.
byOptionId.th: Incorrect â€” <th> is for header cells inside rows.

questions.prompt: Which tag is used for header cells?

explanations.overview: <th> defines a header cell, often bold and centered by default.

byOptionId.th: Correct â€” <th> indicates header cells within a table row.
byOptionId.td: Incorrect â€” <td> is for regular cells, not headers.
byOptionId.tr: Incorrect â€” <tr> is a row wrapper, not a cell.
byOptionId.thead: Incorrect â€” <thead> groups header rows, but <th> is the header cell element.
